title: Cpk to PPM Converter
description: Converts Process Capability Index (Cpk) to Parts Per Million (PPM) defect rate and provides actionable insights for process improvement. Based on Six Sigma and ISO 22514 standards.

inputs.label: Process Capability Index (Cpk)
inputs.businessContext: Cpk is a standard Six Sigma metric indicating how well a process meets specification limits, accounting for centering. Typical targets: 1.33 (minimum acceptable), 1.67 (good), 2.0 (excellent).

businessContext_i18n.en: Cpk is a standard Six Sigma metric indicating how well a process meets specification limits, accounting for centering. Typical targets: 1.33 (minimum acceptable), 1.67 (good), 2.0 (excellent).

inputs.label: Sigma Shift (long-term drift)
inputs.businessContext: Accounts for long-term process drift. Standard Six Sigma assumption is 1.5 sigma shift. Adjust based on historical data or industry norms (e.g., automotive often uses 1.5).

businessContext_i18n.en: Accounts for long-term process drift. Standard Six Sigma assumption is 1.5 sigma shift. Adjust based on historical data or industry norms (e.g., automotive often uses 1.5).

inputs.label: Distribution Type
inputs.businessContext: Select distribution based on sample size and data characteristics. Normal for large samples (n>30), t-distribution for small samples (n<30) to account for uncertainty.

businessContext_i18n.en: Select distribution based on sample size and data characteristics. Normal for large samples (n>30), t-distribution for small samples (n<30) to account for uncertainty.

inputs.label: Sample Size (n)
inputs.businessContext: Number of observations used to estimate Cpk. Larger samples improve confidence. Required for t-distribution adjustment.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of observations used to estimate Cpk. Larger samples improve confidence. Required for t-distribution adjustment.

inputs.label: Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level for the confidence interval of the PPM estimate. Higher levels provide more conservative (wider) intervals.

businessContext_i18n.en: Confidence level for the confidence interval of the PPM estimate. Higher levels provide more conservative (wider) intervals.

inputs.label: Use Confidence Interval
inputs.businessContext: If enabled, the tool calculates lower and upper bounds for PPM based on the confidence level. Useful for risk assessment.

businessContext_i18n.en: If enabled, the tool calculates lower and upper bounds for PPM based on the confidence level. Useful for risk assessment.

rules.message: Cpk values above 3.0 are extremely rare; please verify input.
Sigma shift must be between 0 and 2.0.
Sample size must be at least 2 for t-distribution.
Confidence level must be between 90% and 99% when using confidence interval.

thresholds.message: Cpk below 1.33 indicates process is not capable. Immediate improvement required.
Cpk between 1.33 and 1.67 is acceptable but consider improvement for higher sigma levels.
PPM exceeds 1000; process is producing excessive defects.
PPM between 100 and 1000; monitor process closely.

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
